perturbation.original_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose. Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose. Upon written demand by Discloser only 30 days after Effective Date, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the demand for the return of confidential information to 'only 30 days after the Effective Date' directly contradicts the original clause stating that this demand can be made 'at any time'. The change introduces uncertainty about when and whether the Discloser can reclaim their confidential information, potentially undermining the agreement's confidentiality provisions during its term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose. Upon written demand by Discloser only 30 days after Effective Date, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Discloser can demand the return of confidential information 'at any time'. The changed text modifies this, limiting the demand to 'only 30 days after Effective Date'. This creates a direct contradiction because the Discloser's right to demand the information back is now time-limited, whereas it was previously unlimited. The contradicted text uses the same modified text, highlighting the fact that a clear contradiction was introduced.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not preclude' to 'shall preclude' in Section 8 creates a contradiction. Initially, the agreement allowed the Recipient to work on similar projects if they didn't breach the agreement. Now, it forbids them from pursuing similar projects altogether, regardless of whether they breach the confidentiality terms. This contradicts the overall intent of allowing independent development.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall not preclude' to 'shall preclude' in Section 8 creates a direct contradiction. The original text allowed the Recipient to work on similar projects as long as they didn't breach the agreement or use Confidential Information. The changed text forbids the Recipient from developing similar projects altogether, regardless of a breach. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which means the change is internally contradictory because it says two opposing things. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Except as expressly permitted herein, Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information and shall prevent the disclosure of such information by Recipient or its affiliates and their respective employees, directors, counsel, advisors, agents and independent contractors (all of which collectively "Representatives") by using the same degree of care to keep confidential the Confidential Information as it uses to keep confidential its own confidential information. In addition, except as permitted herein, Recipient shall not disclose the fact that the parties are exchanging Confidential Information and having discussions. In connection therewith, it is agreed that no public release or disclosure of any <PAGE> 2 contemplated transaction shall be made except by a mutually agreed disclosure except that each party may make such disclosure if advised by its outside securities counsel in writing that such disclosure is required; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event such party will notify the other party that it intends, as a preliminary matter, to take such action and the outside securities counsel of such party shall first discuss the mater with the outside securities counsel of the other party before any definitive decision is made on the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Except as expressly permitted herein, Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information and shall prevent the disclosure of such information by Recipient or its affiliates and their respective employees, directors, counsel, advisors, agents and independent contractors (all of which collectively "Representatives") by using the same degree of care to keep confidential its own confidential information. In addition, except as permitted herein, Recipient shall not disclose the fact that the parties are exchanging Confidential Information and having discussions. In connection therewith, it is agreed that no public release or disclosure of any <PAGE> 2 contemplated transaction shall be made except by a mutually agreed disclosure except that each party may make such disclosure if advised by its outside securities counsel in writing that such disclosure is required; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event such party will notify the other party that it intends, as a preliminary matter, to take such action and the outside securities counsel of such party shall first discuss the mater with the outside securities counsel of the other party before any definitive decision is made on the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not disclose' to 'shall disclose' in Section 2 directly contradicts the core principle of a non-disclosure agreement. The purpose of the document is to protect sensitive information, making it unclear what the recipient should actually do with the confidential information. This change makes the purpose of the agreement entirely unclear. It directly contradicts the original intention of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly permitted herein, Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information and shall prevent the disclosure of such information by Recipient or its affiliates and their respective employees, directors, counsel, advisors, agents and independent contractors (all of which collectively "Representatives") by using the same degree of care to keep confidential its own confidential information. In addition, except as permitted herein, Recipient shall not disclose the fact that the parties are exchanging Confidential Information and having discussions. In connection therewith, it is agreed that no public release or disclosure of any <PAGE> 2 contemplated transaction shall be made except by a mutually agreed disclosure except that each party may make such disclosure if advised by its outside securities counsel in writing that such disclosure is required; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event such party will notify the other party that it intends, as a preliminary matter, to take such action and the outside securities counsel of such party shall first discuss the mater with the outside securities counsel of the other party before any definitive decision is made on the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the 'Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information'. The changed text modifies this to 'Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction, rendering the non-disclosure agreement fundamentally contradictory and unenforceable. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, it causes a structural flaw in the document based on conflicting clauses with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipients shall use Confidential Information solely for the purpose (the "Permitted Purpose") specified at the end of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipients may or may not use Confidential Information for the purpose (the "Permitted Purpose") specified at the end of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may or may not' in Section 3 introduces uncertainty about the recipient's obligation to use the confidential information for the Permitted Purpose. The initial clause explicitly states the recipient is obligated to use the information solely for the defined purpose. With the changed clause, the recipient has the option of not using the information for that reason. The Permitted Purpose clauses A and B are now contradicted given this is not an obligation, making the purpose of the agreement ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
PERMITTED PURPOSES
A. The "Permitted Purpose" with respect to Confidential Information disclosed to the Company shall be, to evaluate whether or not to enter into an agreement with Ben & Jerry's relating to a joint venture, other cooperative venture or the like between Ben & Jerry's and the Company.
B. The "Permitted Purpose" with respect to Confidential Information disclosed to Ben & Jerry's shall be: to evaluate whether or not to enter into an agreement with the Company relating to a joint venture, other cooperative venture or the like between Ben & Jerry's and the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that recipients 'shall' use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that recipients 'may or may not' use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose. This directly contradicts clauses A and B of the 'PERMITTED PURPOSES' section, which define the specific and intended use of the confidential information. By making the use optional, the changed text undermines the very purpose defined in the contradicted section, as the parties may now choose to ignore the stipulated Permitted Purpose, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction under structural flaws.
